label: Real Analysis
shortDescription: Rigorous foundations of calculus. Sequences, continuity, differentiation, integration, and metric spaces.
modulesDescription: Read the real analysis lessons in order, or jump directly to the topic you need.
ogTitle: Learn Real Analysis for Free | CalcPath
ogDescription: Master rigorous real analysis: the real number system, sequences and series, limits and continuity with ε-δ definitions, differentiation and integration theorems, Lebesgue theory, metric spaces, and advanced topics — with ELI5 explanations, detailed worked examples, and targeted practice questions.
courseDescription: A complete introduction to real analysis providing the rigorous foundations of calculus. Covers construction and properties of real numbers, sequences and convergence, limits and continuity, the derivative and mean value theorems, Riemann and Lebesgue integration, metric spaces and compactness, plus advanced topics like functions of bounded variation and Fourier series. Each lesson includes intuitive ELI5 breakdowns, detailed worked examples, common pitfalls, and targeted practice questions.

topics.title: Real number
topics.description: The numbers that can be represented as limits of Cauchy sequences of rationals or via Dedekind cuts; they form a complete ordered field.

topics.title: Construction of the real numbers
topics.description: How the reals can be rigorously built from the rationals using equivalence classes of Cauchy sequences or Dedekind cuts.

topics.title: Least-upper-bound property
topics.description: Every nonempty subset of reals that is bounded above has a least upper bound (supremum) in the reals.

topics.title: Archimedean property
topics.description: For any positive real x there exists a natural number n such that n > x; the naturals are not bounded in the reals.

topics.title: Density of the rationals
topics.description: Between any two distinct real numbers there exists a rational number (and also an irrational).

topics.title: Dedekind cut
topics.description: A partition of the rationals into two nonempty sets A, B with all of A less than all of B, used to define a real number.

topics.title: Cardinality of the continuum
topics.description: The cardinality of the set of real numbers is 2^ℵ₀, strictly larger than that of the rationals.

topics.title: Sequence
topics.description: An ordered list of numbers a₁, a₂, a₃, … indexed by the natural numbers.

topics.title: Limit of a sequence
topics.description: A number L such that for every ε > 0 there exists N so that for all n > N, |a_n − L| < ε.

topics.title: Cauchy sequence
topics.description: A sequence where terms become arbitrarily close to each other: for every ε > 0 there is N such that m, n > N implies |a_m − a_n| < ε.

topics.title: Series (mathematics)
topics.description: The sum of the terms of a sequence, studied via its partial sums and convergence behavior.

topics.title: Absolute convergence
topics.description: A series ∑ a_n converges absolutely if ∑ |a_n| converges (which implies ordinary convergence).

topics.title: Power series
topics.description: A series of the form ∑ c_n (x − a)^n whose convergence depends on the distance from the center a.

topics.title: Limit of a function
topics.description: L is the limit of f(x) as x → a if for every ε > 0 there is δ > 0 such that 0 < |x − a| < δ implies |f(x) − L| < ε.

topics.title: (ε, δ)-definition of limit
topics.description: The precise ε-δ formulation that makes 'approaches' rigorous without relying on infinitesimals.

topics.title: Continuous function
topics.description: A function f is continuous at a if lim_{x→a} f(x) = f(a); equivalently, for every ε there is δ with |x−a|<δ ⇒ |f(x)−f(a)|<ε.

topics.title: Uniform continuity
topics.description: A function is uniformly continuous on a set if the δ in the continuity definition can be chosen independently of the point.

topics.title: Intermediate value theorem
topics.description: If f is continuous on [a,b] and k is between f(a) and f(b), then there exists c in (a,b) with f(c)=k.

topics.title: Extreme value theorem
topics.description: A continuous function on a closed bounded interval attains its maximum and minimum values.

topics.title: Derivative
topics.description: The instantaneous rate of change f'(a) = lim_{h→0} [f(a+h) − f(a)] / h when the limit exists.

topics.title: Mean value theorem
topics.description: If f is continuous on [a,b] and differentiable on (a,b), then there is c in (a,b) with f'(c) = [f(b)−f(a)]/(b−a).

topics.title: Taylor's theorem
topics.description: f can be approximated by its Taylor polynomial of degree n plus a remainder term involving the (n+1)th derivative.

topics.title: L'Hôpital's rule
topics.description: If lim f/g is 0/0 or ∞/∞ form, then lim f/g = lim f'/g' under suitable conditions.

topics.title: Riemann integral
topics.description: The limit of Riemann sums ∑ f(x_i*) Δx_i as the partition mesh goes to zero, when the limit exists.

topics.title: Fundamental theorem of calculus
topics.description: If F' = f continuous then ∫_a^b f = F(b) − F(a); differentiation and integration are inverse.

topics.title: Improper integral
topics.description: Integrals over unbounded intervals or with unbounded integrands, defined as limits of proper integrals.

topics.title: Riemann–Stieltjes integral
topics.description: A generalization of Riemann integral using integrator function α, reducing to Riemann when α(x)=x.

topics.title: Lebesgue integral
topics.description: An integral defined via measure theory that allows integration of highly discontinuous functions and has better convergence properties.

topics.title: Dominated convergence theorem
topics.description: If |f_n| ≤ g integrable and f_n → f pointwise, then ∫ f_n → ∫ f and f integrable.

topics.title: Metric space
topics.description: A set X with a distance function d satisfying positivity, symmetry, and triangle inequality.

topics.title: Open set and closed set
topics.description: In a metric space, open sets are unions of open balls; closed sets contain all their limit points.

topics.title: Compact space
topics.description: A space where every open cover has a finite subcover; in ℝ^n equivalent to closed and bounded (Heine-Borel).

topics.title: Complete metric space
topics.description: Every Cauchy sequence converges to a point in the space (e.g., ℝ is complete; ℚ is not).

topics.title: Connected space
topics.description: A space that cannot be written as the union of two disjoint nonempty open sets.

topics.title: Banach fixed-point theorem
topics.description: A contraction mapping on a complete metric space has a unique fixed point, with iterative convergence.

topics.title: Baire category theorem
topics.description: A complete metric space is not a countable union of nowhere-dense sets (has 'generic' points dense).

topics.title: Function of bounded variation
topics.description: A function whose total variation over an interval is finite; can be written as difference of two increasing functions.

topics.title: Absolutely continuous function
topics.description: A function that is an indefinite integral of an integrable function; stronger than bounded variation and continuous.

topics.title: Differentiation of measures
topics.description: The Lebesgue differentiation theorem says that for an integrable function the average over balls converges to the value a.e.

topics.title: Lp space
topics.description: The space of functions (or sequences) whose p-th power is integrable, with norm ||f||_p = (∫ |f|^p)^{1/p}.

topics.title: Fourier series
topics.description: Expansion of a periodic function as sum of sines and cosines (or complex exponentials) with coefficients via integrals.

topics.title: Multivariable calculus (rigorous)
topics.description: Rigorous treatment of partial derivatives, multiple integrals, line and surface integrals, and the theorems of Green, Stokes, and Gauss in ℝ^n.
